name: Slopes of Pontal do Rio Guadiana — circular route (PR2)
name_original: Ladeiras do Pontal do Rio Guadiana — percurso circular (PR2)
region: Alcoutim e Pereiro e Pereiro e Pereiro
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Alcoutim e Pereiro e Pereiro e Pereiro are characterized by the diverse landscape of Portugal's Algarve region. The area features the Guadiana River, forming a natural border and offering riverine paths. The Serra do Caldeirão mountains provide a backdrop, with terrain including cistus plants, holm, oak, and olive trees. Ancient history is present with features like the Menhirs of Lavajo, adding cultural interest to the natural surroundings. Best easy hiking trails around Alcoutim e Pereiro e Pereiro e Pereiro The most popular easy hiking route is The Charms of Alcoutim — circular route (PR3), a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 22 minutes to complete. This circular route offers varied scenery around Alcoutim. Another top favourite among local hikers is Lavajo Menhirs – Shamwari Hilltop Retreat loop from Vascão, an easy 3.8 miles (6.2 km) path. This route passes by the ancient Menhirs of Lavajo and offers views from the Shamwari Hilltop Retreat. Local hikers also love the View of the Guadiana River – Castelo Velho de Alcoutim loop from Sanlúcar de Guadiana, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes and past the historic Castelo Velho de Alcoutim, often completed in about 1 hour 34 minutes. Hiking around Alcoutim e Pereiro e Pereiro e Pereiro is defined by river valleys, rolling hills, and traditional Portuguese landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, focusing on easy hiking trails. The routes in Alcoutim e Pereiro e Pereiro e Pereiro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 190 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Alcoutim e Pereiro e Pereiro e Pereiro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Alcoutim e Pereiro?
A: There are 12 easy hiking trails around Alcoutim e Pereiro, offering diverse scenery from riverine paths to rolling hills. In total, the region features 29 hiking routes across various difficulty levels.

Q: Are there any circular easy hiking routes in Alcoutim e Pereiro?
A: Yes, several easy circular routes are available. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421641788/the-charms-of-alcoutim-circular-route-pr3" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Charms of Alcoutim — circular route (PR3)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, offering varied scenery over 4.8 km. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10318329" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavajo Menhirs – Shamwari Hilltop Retreat loop from Vascão&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.2 km long.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see on easy hikes in Alcoutim e Pereiro?
A: Easy hikes in Alcoutim e Pereiro often feature the majestic Guadiana River, which forms a natural border with Spain, offering stunning river views. You'll also encounter the backdrop of the Serra do Caldeirão mountains, with landscapes of cistus plants, holm, oak, and olive trees. The region is also home to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4080009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana River&lt;/a&gt; itself, and you might spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4844541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cork Oak Forests of the Algarve&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the easy trails?
A: Absolutely. Many easy trails pass by significant historical and cultural sites. You can explore the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5313767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavajo Menhirs&lt;/a&gt;, dating back to the Late Neolithic or Chalcolithic period. Some routes also lead past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6130909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelo Velho de Alcoutim&lt;/a&gt;, an archaeological site showcasing Islamic military architecture, or the medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5597192" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelo de Alcoutim&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for easy hikes in the area?
A: Easy hikes in Alcoutim e Pereiro typically range from 4.8 km to just over 6 km. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421641788/the-charms-of-alcoutim-circular-route-pr3" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Charms of Alcoutim — circular route (PR3)&lt;/a&gt; is about 4.8 km and takes around 1 hour 22 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10318329" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavajo Menhirs – Shamwari Hilltop Retreat loop from Vascão&lt;/a&gt; is 6.2 km.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Alcoutim e Pereiro?
A: The easy trails in Alcoutim e Pereiro are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 190 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility, and the well-maintained paths that showcase the region's natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there any viewpoints on the easy hiking trails?
A: Yes, several easy routes offer scenic viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14582590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontal Viewpoint – Lake View loop from Baixo Guadiana Footpaths&lt;/a&gt; includes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4546209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontal Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, providing expansive vistas. Another popular spot is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint over the Guadiana River&lt;/a&gt;, which can be enjoyed on various trails.

Q: Can I find trails that follow the Guadiana River?
A: Yes, the Guadiana River is a central feature of the region, and many trails offer riverine paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17265600" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Guadiana River – Castelo Velho de Alcoutim loop from Sanlúcar de Guadiana&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, leading you through picturesque riverine landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy trails suitable for families with children?
A: The easy trails in Alcoutim e Pereiro are generally suitable for families, given their moderate distances and minimal elevation gain. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421641788/the-charms-of-alcoutim-circular-route-pr3" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Charms of Alcoutim — circular route (PR3)&lt;/a&gt; are relatively short and offer engaging scenery, making them good options for a family outing.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hiking in Alcoutim e Pereiro?
A: The region is pleasant for hiking for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures, ideal for exploring. Even in winter, the riverbeds transform from rushing torrents to shimmering pools, offering unique scenery. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during that season.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or river beaches accessible via easy hikes?
A: While the Pulo do Lobo (Wolf's Leap) is a notable waterfall on the Guadiana, it might require a slightly more adventurous approach. However, for a relaxing break, the tranquil Praia Fluvial do Pego Fundo, a local river beach, is a popular spot that can be a destination or a stop on some routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on easy hikes?
A: The easy hikes in Alcoutim e Pereiro feature diverse terrain, including river valleys, rolling hills, and traditional Portuguese landscapes. Paths are generally well-defined, passing through areas with cistus plants, holm, oak, and olive trees, offering a mix of natural and cultivated scenery.
tips: Bularco de Baixo is a small village where the route towards the Guadiana River begins. There is little to do there, besides the parking lot, water fountain and the occasional place to buy a few things.
This is a stretch of dirt road that leads to the Guadiana River. The route passes through hills and, at each section, there are views of the entire region. Very peaceful.

At the eastern end of the route, the road follows the Guadiana River. In this same area there is the Miradouro do Pontal. From here you have a beautiful and privileged view of Rio. Great stopping point before starting the return journey.

waypoints.name: Dirt Road to the Guadiana River
